message:
feat(conformance): add conformance testing infrastructure

This commit introduces a comprehensive conformance testing framework that
enables running the same test suites against different runtime implementations
(Lab, production, etc.) to ensure consistent behavioral guarantees.

## Core Components

### TestConfig (lines 34-116)
Builder-pattern configuration for conformance tests:
- timeout: Maximum test duration (default: 30s)
- rng_seed: Optional seed for deterministic execution (default: 0xDEAD_BEEF)
- tracing_enabled: Toggle detailed tracing during test execution
- max_steps: Step limit for Lab runtime to prevent infinite loops (default: 100k)
- root_budget: Budget allocated to the root region

### ConformanceTarget trait (lines 230-293)
Abstract interface that runtime implementations must fulfill:
- create_runtime(): Factory method respecting TestConfig
- block_on(): Run futures to completion on the runtime
- spawn(): Task spawning within the current region with budget tracking
- create_region(): Child region creation for hierarchical structure
- cancel(): Initiate cancellation protocol on a region
- advance_time(): Virtual time advancement (Lab) or sleep (production)
- is_quiescent(): Check if runtime has reached quiescence
- now(): Query current virtual/wall-clock time

### TaskHandle<T> and RegionHandle (lines 118-193)
Type-safe handles for async coordination:
- TaskHandle wraps spawned task futures with Outcome<T, ()> semantics
- RegionHandle wraps region lifecycle with Future completion signaling
- Both implement Future for ergonomic .await usage

### LabRuntimeTarget (lines 371-474)
Reference implementation of ConformanceTarget for the deterministic Lab runtime:
- Integrates with LabConfig for seed, max_steps, and trace_capacity
- block_on() creates root region, spawns task, runs until quiescence
- advance_time() delegates to LabRuntime's virtual clock

### conformance_test! macro (lines 359-369)
Declarative test definition that auto-generates #[test] functions:
- Creates TestConfig with defaults
- Passes config to test body closure
- Enables portable test definitions across runtime targets

### run_conformance_tests() (lines 307-335)
Test runner with panic recovery and result reporting:
- Catches panics to allow continued execution after failures
- Prints PASSED/FAILED status with error details
- Returns (passed, failed) counts for summary

## Design Rationale

The framework enables "write once, verify everywhere" testing where oracle-based
conformance tests written for the Lab runtime can later be validated against
production implementations without modification.

Co-Authored-By: LilacPuma <lilacpuma@agent.local>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
